methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $690M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bethlehem Steel Corp is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $690M to date. The broader sector comprises 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage matches with similar valuation profiles to support a reliable peer-median approach.

The Stage-Progression method is the only applicable triangulation approach given current data constraints. This method applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $690M total raised proxy, reflecting typical valuation uplift from Growth stage to subsequent rounds. This yields an implied valuation of $828M. A sensitivity analysis around this multiplier—testing downside scenarios (1.15x) and upside scenarios (1.38x)—produces a valuation range of $704M to $952M.

The analysis supports a median valuation of $828M, with a plausible range of $704M–$952M. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-peer comparable data; a more granular peer-median analysis would require identification of same-stage robotics companies with disclosed valuations. Additionally, the reliance on total raised as a valuation proxy assumes no material secondary transactions or mark-downs have occurred post-fundraising. Public market comparables and deeper private peer analysis would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
